subject: Weekly FOIA log + one request needing your sign-off
body_clean: Mayor, here is this week's FOIA log. Three new requests came in: FOIA-2026-0142 (Tribune stringer, all police overtime records Jan-May), FOIA-2026-0143 (resident requesting water-meter installation records for 1042 25th Ave), and FOIA-2026-0144 (a law firm requesting all emails between the Village and Halvorsen Paving re the Washington Blvd contract). The first two are routine and I'll respond by the statutory deadline of June 26. The Halvorsen request (0144) is voluminous and may touch attorney-client material — I'd like your sign-off before I invoke the 5-business-day extension. Can you confirm by tomorrow?

subject: Weekly FOIA log + one request needing your sign-off
body_clean: Thanks. Go ahead and invoke the extension on FOIA-2026-0144 — Halvorsen is exactly the kind of request where I want our attorney to review for privilege before anything goes out. Loop in counsel this week. The other two, send on the normal timeline. Please flag any request that mentions the Washington Blvd stormwater project directly to me; I want to stay ahead of it.

subject: Q2 billing cycle closing + a high-bill adjustment for your awareness
body_clean: Mayor, the Q2 water/sewer billing cycle closes Friday June 26 and bills mail July 1, due July 31. Consumption is up about 9% over Q2 last year, consistent with the dry June. One item for your awareness: Eleanor Meyer at 1733 Frederick Ave received a $612 bill — roughly 4x her normal — after a slab leak. Per the leak-adjustment policy I'm crediting the sewer portion (since the water didn't enter the sanitary system) once she submits the plumber's repair receipt. Estimated adjustment is about $190. She was upset on the phone but understood once I walked her through it. No action needed unless she escalates to you.

subject: Permit BP-2026-0388 issued — restaurant buildout at 612 St. Charles (El Faro)
body_clean: Mayor, good news on the corridor: I issued building permit BP-2026-0388 today for El Faro's outdoor patio enclosure and interior kitchen-hood work at 612 St. Charles. Plan review took longer than I'd like — the original grease-duct detail didn't meet the mechanical code and they had to revise — but the corrected set passed. Rough-in inspections are scheduled for the week of July 6. The owner has been asking about timing for Taste of Bellwood, so I let them know the patio framing can proceed now. Just wanted you to have this since you'd heard from them directly.

subject: Failed reinspection at 2218 Bohland — deck permit BP-2026-0351
body_clean: Mayor, flagging this only because the homeowner is Marcus Webb, who I know writes to your office. His rear deck at 2218 Bohland Ave (permit BP-2026-0351) failed its framing inspection a second time today — the ledger board is lap-bolted into the rim joist without proper flashing and the footings are above frost depth. I gave him a written correction list and offered a free reinspection once the contractor fixes it. He was frustrated but I can't pass framing that isn't to code; this is a safety item. If he reaches out to you, that's the background.

subject: Notice of violation issued — 588 St. Charles (weeds/grass + open dumping)
body_clean: Mayor, I issued a notice of violation today, CE-2026-0274, for the vacant building at 588 St. Charles. Grass and weeds well over the 8-inch limit under Chapter 50, plus a pile of construction debris dumped along the rear wall since last week. The registered owner has 10 days to abate or the Village will mow and bill the cost as a lien, per ordinance. I've also referred the dumping to Public Works for cleanup quote. Given the corridor visibility and Taste of Bellwood traffic next month, I recommend we move quickly to the abatement step if they don't comply.

subject: Taste of Bellwood + summer parks prep — status and two decisions needed
body_clean: Mayor, Taste of Bellwood is set for the weekend of July 18-19 in Bellwood Park. Eleven food vendors are confirmed and the stage/sound contract is signed. Two things need your decision this week: (1) PD is recommending we close 25th Ave between St. Charles and Washington both days for the vendor footprint — that needs your nod for the street-closure resolution on the July 6 board agenda; and (2) the splash pad and the two ballfields need their summer turn-on, but the splash pad failed its first water-quality test (chlorine feed pump) and the repair part is on order. I expect the splash pad open by July 1 — before the festival. Pool concession reopens June 27.

subject: Taste of Bellwood + summer parks prep — status and two decisions needed
body_clean: Great progress. Yes to the 25th Ave closure — put the street-closure resolution on the July 6 consent agenda and coordinate the detour signage with Public Works. On the splash pad, keep me posted weekly; if the chlorine pump part slips past July 1, I want a backup plan before families show up for the festival. Make sure we have the certificate of insurance from every vendor on file before opening day — no exceptions. Nice work pulling eleven vendors together.

subject: Purchase order over threshold needs your authorization — PO-2026-0511 (mower replacement)
body_clean: Mayor, Parks has requested replacement of the 2011 Toro out-front mower that's been down twice this month. The low quote from the state purchasing cooperative (Sourcewell) is $31,480, which exceeds the $25,000 administrative threshold and needs either your authorization or board approval. Because it's a cooperative contract, you can authorize it under the competitive-bid exemption without a full RFP — I've attached the memo. There's room in the parks capital line; this won't affect the general fund. If you sign PO-2026-0511 this week, delivery is 3-4 weeks, in time to keep the ballfields and festival grounds mowed. Otherwise it goes to the July 6 board.

subject: Purchase order over threshold needs your authorization — PO-2026-0511 (mower replacement)
body_clean: Approved — I'll sign PO-2026-0511 today. Using the Sourcewell cooperative contract is the right call; document the competitive-bid exemption in the file so it's clean if anyone FOIAs it. Confirm with the vendor that delivery lands before July 18 so we're not scrambling for the festival. Please also include this as an information item in the July 6 board packet so the trustees see it even though it didn't need their vote.

subject: Washington Blvd stormwater capital project — 60% design + grant update
body_clean: Mayor, status on the Washington Blvd / Frederick Ave stormwater relief project (CP-2026-04): the consultant delivered the 60% design this week. The model confirms the new 36-inch trunk storm sewer and the underground detention vault under the village lot will cut the 10-year flood elevation at Frederick and 17th by about 14 inches — that's the block that floods every heavy rain. Construction estimate is now $2.4M, up from the $2.1M planning figure due to a watermain conflict we found. Good news: our IEPA 319 grant application scored well and we're likely to land roughly $900K toward it. I'll have the 60% cost breakdown and the revised schedule for the July 6 committee meeting.

subject: Storm-sewer televising results — 1042 25th Ave area
body_clean: Mayor, following up on the repeat backyard flooding near Gloria Bennett's property at 1042 25th Ave. We televised the 12-inch storm line on 25th between Rice and Washington last week. The camera found two partial collapses and heavy root intrusion at roughly 140 feet — enough to choke flow in a hard rain, which matches the complaints. This is a maintenance fix, not a capital project: I'm recommending a cured-in-place pipe (CIPP) liner for the bad segment, about $48,000, which Public Works can fund from the storm-maintenance line this year. I can have a spot work order ready for your review by Friday. This should resolve Ms. Bennett's recurring issue.
